questions.what: Direction was wrong: predicted down but price rose.
questions.why: Contrarian/sell-alert signal over-interpreted a local pullback as trend.
questions.rule: Require a confirming second signal before taking a down position when SELL ALERT is the primary driver.
questions.devil: To predict the opposite outcome I would have needed to believe that SELL ALERT was already priced in and that buyers were exhausted, favouring a up move instead.

questions.what: Direction was wrong: predicted down but price rose.

questions.devil: To predict the opposite outcome I would have needed to believe that SELL ALERT was already priced in and that buyers were exhausted, favouring a up move instead.

questions.what: Direction was wrong: predicted up but price fell.
questions.why: Supporting evidence was incomplete or stale relative to actual price action.
questions.rule: Require a confirming second signal before taking a up position when up is the primary driver.
questions.devil: To predict the opposite outcome I would have needed to believe that the dip was overdone, support was holding, and risk/reward favoured a down reversal instead.
